task: I need to send a short note to a client about a delay, and I want to make sure it covers the key point without creating extra back-and-forth. Can you help me draft a simple message that gets the update across clearly?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a simple version:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the deliverable is running behind schedule because of [brief reason]. I expect to send it by [new date/time].

I’ll keep you posted if anything changes.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make it a little firmer or more polished.

user_simulator.text: Close, but I’d want it a little tighter and less vague. I don’t want it sounding like I’m making excuses. Give me one version that’s plain and professional, maybe a touch firmer, and doesn’t invite a lot of follow-up. Something I can send as-is.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version you can send as-is:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the deliverable is delayed due to [brief reason]. I’m working to get it to you by [new date/time].

I’ll let you know if that changes.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even firmer or more specific to the situation.

user_simulator.text: Closer. Still a little soft. I’d rather it say the update plain, without the “working to get it” part if possible. Something more like: the job will be sent by [new date/time], no extra explanation. If you can tighten it one more notch, that’s the version I want.

turns.agent_response:
Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the deliverable is delayed and will be sent by [new date/time].

Best,  
[Your Name]

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the one. Plain and clean. I’d send it.
